channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘Love Story’ Has People Falling for ’90s New York City Again
item.description: The show, about John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette, has fans flocking to restaurants and stores the couple visited during their romance.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Loggins

media:content.media:credit: Lanna Apisukh for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Boshir Khan, right, the owner of Panna II, an Indian restaurant in the East Village, has lines out the door since “Love Story” began airing on FX.

item.title: ‘I Jumped Out of Bed, Marched Next Door and Began an Irrational Rant’
item.description: Confronting a noisy neighbor, at a Manhattan club after the prom and more reader tales of New York City in this week’s Metropolitan Diary.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 22, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: EE. UU. e Israel empiezan a revelar prioridades diferentes en Irán
item.description: Estados Unidos ve a Irán desde la perspectiva de sus responsabilidades globales y de sus objetivos estratégicos. Israel tiene un enfoque más regional. Después de casi tres semanas de guerra, sus caminos se están separando.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Refinerías que sirven al yacimiento de gas de South Pars en Asaluyeh, Irán, en 2019. Las instalaciones, que procesan la mayor parte del gas natural iraní para consumo interno, resultaron dañadas en un ataque el miércoles.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Threatens Iran’s Power Plants if Strait of Hormuz Does Not Open
item.description: President Trump’s ultimatum comes after Iranian missiles struck two cities in southern Israel, including one near the country’s main nuclear research center.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: The End of the Free-Range ‘Stand by Me’ Childhood
item.description: What a 40-year-old movie told me about childhood today.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Wildman

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; photographs by Columbia Pictures and Ackun/Getty Images

item.title: Change Your Life. Start a Band.
item.description: We crave connection. We crave community. You should start by recruiting a drummer.
item.dc:creator: Hugo Lindgren

item.title: Trump Has Made a Fundamental Miscalculation about Iran
item.description: America shouldn’t fight a war like this.
item.dc:creator: Phil Klay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: ¿Un psicodélico poco conocido puede ayudar a tratar el trauma?
item.description: Veteranos y otras personas que han sufrido traumas y lesiones están viajando a clínicas de todo el mundo para tomar ibogaína. Yo también emprendí ese viaje por una razón profundamente personal.
item.dc:creator: Robert Draper

item.title: Quote of the Day: A Lifeline for Inmates Turns Deadly
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, March 22, 2026.

item.title: Election in Slovenia Will Test Europe’s Political Mood
item.description: The center-left government is being challenged by a right-wing populist accused of launching a smear campaign in the final days of a close race.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins

media:content.media:description: Slovenia’s opposition leader and nationalist former Prime Minister Janez Jansa prepares for the last televised political debate in Ljubljana on Friday, ahead of the parliamentary elections.

item.title: A Brussels Community Moves on From Terrorist Attacks, but New Challenges Emerge
item.description: Molenbeek was branded a hotbed of radicalization after the Paris and Brussels terrorist attacks of a decade ago. It is trying to reinvent itself amid new challenges.
item.dc:creator: Koba Ryckewaert and Jeanna Smialek

media:content.media:credit: Jim Huylebroek for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graffiti on one of the bridges over the canal dividing Molenbeek and the city of Brussels in March.

item.title: France Is Voting for Thousands of New Mayors. Here’s Why It Matters.
item.description: The far-right party had first-round successes in several cities, but so did the far left. Sunday’s second round may clarify who has real momentum.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler

media:content.media:credit: James Hill for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Campaign posters outside a school in Marseille, France.

item.title: The Billionaire Funding France’s Far Right
item.description: Pierre-Édouard Stérin is financing projects to make France less Muslim, more Catholic and more capitalist. He says his program has trained thousands running for municipal office on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter

media:content.media:credit: James Hill for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pierre-Édouard Stérin at his chalet in the French Alps on Wednesday. He moved his family to Belgium in 2012.

item.title: Who Is Antigone? The 2500-Year-Old Rebel With a Cause.
item.description: “Antigone” gave us the original “bad girl,” but its themes go beyond that. How do adaptations keep making Sophocles’ ideas about democracy and theater new?
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

item.title: Why Are We Obsessed With Antigone?
item.description: Antigone, an ancient Greek play, is being adapted in several theaters across New York City. Our critic Helen Shaw explains why Sophocles’s anti-heroine is such a relevant figure today.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw, Léo Hamelin and Laura Salaberry

item.title: Italy’s Leader Wants to Change the Constitution. Italians Don’t Get It.
item.description: Voting starts Sunday on a referendum to overhaul Italy’s judiciary. Many people don’t understand the complex proposal — and it could hurt Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich and Josephine de La Bruyère

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy waving to supporters at a campaign event this month.

item.title: As War Disrupts India’s Gulf Ties, Economy Faces ‘New Broadside’
item.description: Generations of Indian workers and businesses have turned the Arab side of the Gulf into a vital appendage of their own country.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Migrant workers taking a break in Dubai this month.

item.title: Corrections: March 22, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, March 22, 2026.

item.title: Beshear Criticizes Vance in Ohio, Stoking 2028 Presidential Tensions
item.description: Mr. Beshear, the Democratic governor of Kentucky, sharply criticized Mr. Vance as both angle for potential presidential runs.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: Gov. Andy Beshear’s rivalry with the vice president goes back years.

item.title: Trump’s Reaction to Mueller’s Death: ‘Good, I’m Glad.’
item.description: The president has been known to disparage foes after their deaths. His response to Mr. Mueller’s death drew criticism from Republicans and Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates

media:content.media:description: Robert S. Mueller III appeared before the House Intelligence Committee in 2019.

item.title: Mullin Explored Bipartisan Deal to Rein in Immigration Crackdown
item.description: The G.O.P. senator President Trump chose to lead the Department of Homeland Security privately discussed concessions the White House has repeatedly rejected.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:description: Senator Markwayne Mullin had been quietly working to hash out a compromise on immigration enforcement that offered more ground than the White House had publicly given.

item.title: News Outlets Pressure Pentagon to Restore Access After Court Ruling
item.description: A federal judge ruled on Friday that key parts of the Pentagon’s press policy were unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Journalists leaving the Pentagon last year after news outlets declined to sign the Defense Department’s restrictive media rules.

item.title: Maui Braces for Storm as Oahu Lifts Evacuation Orders
item.description: Thousands of residents on Oahu were allowed to return home on Saturday afternoon as the storm moved to Maui.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:description: A flooded neighborhood in Waialua, Hawaii, on Friday.

item.title: Former F.B.I. Director Robert Mueller Dies at 81
item.description: Robert Mueller, who led the Federal Bureau of Investigation for 12 tumultuous years, brought politically explosive indictments as a special counsel examining Russia’s attack on the 2016 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:description: Robert S. Mueller III in 2019, while serving as special counsel investigating Russia’s attack on the 2016 presidential election.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: An Iranian missile wounded dozens in Dimona, a city believed to house Israel’s nuclear weapons program, as Iran showed no signs of backing down.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: An Israeli soldier uses a flashlight while walking in the ruins of a damaged building in the southern Israeli city of Dimona.

item.title: Video of Justin Timberlake’s Traffic Stop in 2024 Released
item.description: Body-camera footage of Justin Timberlake’s traffic stop was released on Friday. The singer pleaded guilty to a low-level charge of driving while impaired in Sag Harbor, N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Sag Harbor Police Department, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A still image from the body-camera footage released this week by the Sag Harbor Police Department.

item.title: Sound It Out
item.description: Alex Eaton-Salners presents a conversational Sunday puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Maurizio Gambarini/DPA, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A dandelion, bein’ seedy.

item.title: What Was the Mueller Investigation? And What Did It Find?
item.description: The appointment of Robert S. Mueller III as special counsel to conduct an investigation related to a sitting president roiled Washington around the start of President Trump’s first term.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker and Aimee Ortiz
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates

media:content.media:description: Robert S. Mueller III in his role as special counsel at the Department of Justice in Washington, in 2019.

item.title: ‘We’re Finally Here’: BTS Returns With Sold-Out Concert and New Album
item.description: The K-pop supergroup BTS performed their first concert in over three years in Seoul on Saturday following the release of their new album, “Arirang,” on Friday. Thousands of fans came out to hear one of the biggest names in K-pop.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva, Jiawei Wang and Nailah Morgan

item.title: Trump’s Contested Campus Antisemitism Fight Is Accelerating Again
item.description: While some Jewish students and faculty members have applauded the Trump administration’s renewed push against antisemitism, others believe the government has different motives.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder, Mark Arsenault and Michael C. Bender

media:content.media:credit: Lucy Lu for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Harvard University campus in Cambridge, Mass.

item.title: G.O.P. Bid to Target Transgender Athletes Falls Flat in the Senate
item.description: In moving to bar transgender women and girls from women’s and girls’ sports, Republicans sought to put Democrats in an awkward political spot on a sensitive issue ahead of the midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:description: Senate Republicans failed to attach two provisions targeting transgender Americans to their voter ID bill on Saturday.

item.title: Body-Cam Footage of Justin Timberlake’s DWI Arrest Is Released
item.description: A glassy-eyed and slurring Mr. Timberlake alternates between disbelief, annoyance and occasional humor during his 2024 arrest in Sag Harbor, N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman

media:content.media:credit: Sag Harbor Police Department, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A still image from the body-camera footage released this week by the Sag Harbor Police Department.

item.title: How Corey Lewandowski Wielded Power Inside D.H.S.
item.description: While he was supposed to serve in a limited role as an adviser to Kristi Noem, Mr. Lewandowski had wide influence over contracts, personnel and operations inside the agency over the last year.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz, Alexandra Berzon, Nicholas Nehamas, Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Tyler Pager

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times

item.title: Trump Is Finally Eyeing an Exit From Iran. But Will He Take It?
item.description: President Trump says he is considering “winding down” operations in Iran. But many of his original war goals remain unaccomplished.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The repercussions of President Trump’s “excursion” into Iran may outlast his interest in it.

item.title: With Fire and Song, Kurds in Turkey Hail the Start of Spring
item.description: The Newroz holiday comes at a critical time, as Kurds in Turkey push for peace while their brethren in Syria and Iran fear further conflict.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard, Safak Timur and Rena Effendi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Kurds celebrating Newroz, an ancient holiday that marks the start of spring in the northern hemisphere, in Diyarbakir, Turkey.

item.title: Kendra Duggar, Wife of ‘19 Kids and Counting’ Star, Faces Child Endangerment Charges
item.description: Ms. Duggar was arrested two days after Joseph Duggar, a onetime star of the reality TV series “19 Kids and Counting,” was arrested in a sex abuse case. She appeared in the “Counting On” spinoff.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:description: Kendra Duggar was released from the Washington County Detention Center in Fayetteville, Ark., on Friday.

item.title: ICE Agents to Be Sent to U.S. Airports on Monday, Trump Says
item.description: The president issued a threat to deploy ICE agents in an apparent attempt to force Democrats to approve a new budget for the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Michael Gold

item.title: Charges Dropped Against Woman Mistaken for Protester in Minnesota Church Case
item.description: Federal prosecutors dismissed charges against one of 39 defendants they charged for disrupting a worship service in January to protest the immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cities Church in St. Paul, Minn., where activists disrupted a service in January.

item.title: Patriarch Filaret Dies at 97, Orthodox Church of Ukraine Says
item.description: Filaret led a movement in Ukraine that created a schism among Orthodox believers and, ultimately, a church independent of Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd

media:content.media:credit: Nicole Tung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Patriarch Filaret, center, at St. Volodymyr’s Cathedral in Kyiv, Ukraine, in June 2022. President Volodymyr Zelensky called him “one of the most steadfast defenders” of Ukrainian statehood.

item.title: CBS News Radio Crackles to a Close
item.description: A stalwart of the mass media century, the longstanding network that launched the careers of journalists like Edward R. Murrow will air its final broadcast in May.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum and Benjamin Mullin

media:content.media:description: The pioneering CBS journalists Edward R. Murrow, left, and William Shirer delivered riveting reports from Europe during World War II.

item.title: El regreso de BTS rinde homenaje a sus raíces
item.description: El título del nuevo álbum, ‘Arirang’, es el nombre de un himno popular coreano que simboliza la resistencia y el orgullo nacional.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young, Yan Zhuang, Jeff Benjamin and Max Kim

item.title: Robert Mueller, Former FBI Director Who Led Trump Inquiry, Dies at 81
item.description: He imposed the most significant overhaul of the F.B.I. in its history. Later, after concluding that Russia had interfered in the 2016 election, he became a target of the president’s anger.
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates

media:content.media:description: Robert S. Mueller III in 2019, while serving as special counsel investigating Russia’s attack on the 2016 presidential election.

item.title: Internet Blackout Keeps Iranians From Reaching Loved Ones During Nowruz Holiday
item.description: The current restrictions on the internet in Iran are coinciding with Nowruz, the observance of the Persian New Year.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Shoppers in Tehran prepare for Nowruz, the Persian New Year, even as travel difficulties and an internet blackout keep families apart.

item.title: In Trump’s Administration, Military Housing Is Becoming a Hot Commodity
item.description: Until President Trump took office for the second time, it was rare for civilian government officials to live on military bases or installations.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: At least two members of the Trump administration are living at Fort McNair in Washington.

item.title: Amazon MGM Gets a Much-Needed Hit With ‘Project Hail Mary’
item.description: The movie, which stars Ryan Gosling, is on pace to be the company’s highest-grossing domestic film.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Sperling

media:content.media:credit: Jonathan Olley/Amazon MGM Studios, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Ryan Gosling stars in “Project Hail Mary,” which is based on a novel about the relationship between a reluctant hero and an alien.

item.title: Iran Fires Missiles at Far-Off Diego Garcia Base, but U.S. Remains Out of Range
item.description: Iran’s attempted attack on a military base 2,500 miles away raises questions of the reach of its arsenal.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: Center for Strategic and International Studies

media:content.media:description: Diego Garcia, the site of a joint British-U.S. military base, seen in a U.S. Navy handout photo.

item.title: Paul Brainerd Dies at 78; Pioneered Desktop Publishing With PageMaker
item.description: His software brought printing into the digital age, allowing users to stop manually splicing columns of text and graphics and instead create layouts on a virtual pasteboard.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Rosenwald

item.title: How the Litani River Shapes the Israel-Hezbollah War
item.description: The 90-mile waterway, back in focus amid Israel’s renewed offensive in Lebanon, could define how far the fighting spreads.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Damage to a bridge over the Litani river in southern Lebanon after it was struck on Wednesday.

item.title: Iranian Missiles Evade Israel’s Formidable Defenses, Hitting 2 Cities
item.description: Iranian missiles struck Dimona and Arad in southern Israel, injuring dozens of people. Iran’s state media said the attack on Dimona was aimed at Israel’s nuclear facilities, which U.N. officials said were not hit.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
